definition: The path factor of directed effort that prevents and abandons the unwholesome while developing and sustaining the wholesome.
notes: The project prefers right effort because it is clear and familiar, while right energy remains available in prose that wants to highlight vitality rather than strain. Right striving is discouraged because it can sound forced or ascetic in a way that distorts the factor.

context_rules.context: In most path-factor and training contexts
In explanatory prose emphasizing vitality or ongoing application
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when it clarifies tone.

context_rules.context: In prose stressing contextual fitness rather than willpower
context_rules.notes: Use when the line benefits from softer emphasis.

example_phrases.translation: for the non-arising of unwholesome qualities that have not arisen
example_phrases.notes: One of the core functions of the factor.
